 {"id":"caam-1xv","title":"Research and implement Claude setup-token for headless auth","description":"# Research and Implement Claude Headless Auth

## Research Findings (Completed)

### Available Methods

#### 1. setup-token + CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN (Primary)
```bash
# Generate token
claude setup-token
# Returns: your-oauth-token-here

# Use in headless environment  
export CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN="your-oauth-token-here"
claude -p "your prompt"
```

**Known Issue**: GitHub #8938 reports the token doesn't fully bypass the startup interactive flow. User still sees theme/auth prompts even with token set.

#### 2. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (API Key Mode)
```bash
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..."
claude -p "your prompt"
```
Works for print mode (-p), may not work for interactive sessions.

#### 3. Credential File Transfer (Workaround)
Transfer ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json to remote machine:
```bash
scp ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json user@server:~/.config/claude-code/auth.json
```

#### 4. SSH Port Forwarding (Workaround)
```bash
# Local machine
ssh -L 8080:localhost:8080 user@remote-server
# On remote, run claude /login, open the localhost URL locally
```

### Current Status in Claude Code

- No native device code flow (like codex --device-auth)
- setup-token exists but has bugs (#8938)
- Credential transfer is the most reliable workaround
- GitHub #7100 requests proper headless auth documentation

### Implementation Path for caam

1. **AuthModeSetupToken**: New auth mode using CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
2. **Token Import**: Command to import setup-token result
3. **Credential Export/Import**: Enhanced backup/restore for headless transfer

### Files to Modify

- internal/provider/claude/claude.go
- Add setupToken flow that:
  a. Checks for CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env var
  b. If not set, prompts user to run \`claude setup-token\` elsewhere
  c. Stores token in profile for future use

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Document current limitations clearly
- [ ] Implement token-based auth if feasible
- [ ] Fallback: Provide clear instructions for credential transfer via caam backup/restore

### References

- https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/8938
- https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/7100
- https://claude-did-this.com/claude-hub/getting-started/setup-container-guide","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:37:22.089377565-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:10.248176035-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:10.248176035-05:00","close_reason":"Research complete. Claude doesn't have reliable device-code. setup-token has bugs (GitHub #8938). Recommendation: Use vault transfer approach (caam export/import) which works universally."}
 {"id":"caam-234","title":"Usage Analytics","description":"# Usage Analytics

## Purpose
Track profile usage activity to give users visibility into their patterns and help identify underutilized or overutilized profiles.

## Background (from codex-pool)
codex-pool tracks detailed usage with BoltDB:
- Per-request logs (debugging)
- Per-account aggregates (dashboard)
- Automatic pruning (retention policy)

## Adaptation for caam
caam does not proxy requests, so we cannot track API usage directly. Instead, we track **caam activity**:
- Profile activations
- Login/refresh events
- Errors encountered
- Session durations

## What This Epic Delivers
1. SQLite database setup and schema
2. Activity logging API
3. Session duration tracking
4. Usage aggregation queries
5. CLI usage command
6. TUI usage panel (optional)

## Why SQLite (not BoltDB)
- Query flexibility (complex aggregations)
- Familiar tooling (sqlite3 CLI, DB browsers)
- Mature ecosystem
- CGO-free drivers available (modernc.org/sqlite)

## Database Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE activity_log (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,
    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    provider TEXT NOT NULL,
    profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    details TEXT,
    duration_seconds INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE profile_stats (
    provider TEXT NOT NULL,
    profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    total_activations INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    total_errors INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    total_active_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    last_activated DATETIME,
    last_error DATETIME,
    PRIMARY KEY (provider, profile_name)
);
```

## Event Types
- `activate` - Profile activated
- `deactivate` - Profile deactivated (implicit when switching)
- `login` - User logged in
- `refresh` - Token refreshed
- `error` - Error occurred
- `switch` - Switched from one profile to another

## Retention Policy
- Keep detailed logs for 30 days (configurable)
- Keep aggregates indefinitely
- Auto-prune on startup

## User Experience
```bash
$ caam usage
Profile Usage (last 7 days)
───────────────────────────
claude/work         42 sessions   18.5 hrs
claude/personal     12 sessions    4.2 hrs
codex/main          28 sessions   12.1 hrs

$ caam usage --profile claude/work
Session History for claude/work (last 7 days)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
2025-12-17 09:15  2.3 hrs  (active)
2025-12-16 14:20  1.8 hrs
2025-12-16 09:00  3.1 hrs
```

## Dependencies
- None (parallel to health features)

## Future Enhancements
- Export to CSV/JSON
- Integration with provider usage APIs
- Cost tracking (if providers expose this)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:16:31.226242657-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:44:47.338639408-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:44:47.338639408-05:00","close_reason":"Fully implemented: SQLite db package (db.go, migrations.go), activity logging (events.go), session tracking (session.go), usage CLI command with table/json/csv output. All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-234","depends_on_id":"caam-3am","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:26:48.554681767-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-3am","title":"Smart Profile Management (codex-pool inspired)","description":"# Smart Profile Management Epic

## Origin & Inspiration
This epic brings intelligence from [codex-pool](https://github.com/darvell/codex-pool) to caam. codex-pool is a sophisticated proxy that load-balances requests across multiple AI accounts with automatic failover, token refresh, and usage tracking.

**Key Insight**: While codex-pool answers "which account for THIS request?", caam answers "which account for my WORK SESSION?" The intelligence transfers, but the application differs.

## Goals
1. **Prevent auth failures** through proactive token refresh
2. **Surface profile health** so users can make informed choices
3. **Track activity** for visibility into usage patterns
4. **Reduce friction** with hot reload and project associations

## Non-Goals (for now)
- Proxy mode (request interception)
- Automatic profile switching mid-session
- Rate limit detection from API responses

## Success Metrics
- Zero unexpected auth failures due to token expiry
- Users can identify problematic profiles at a glance
- Usage patterns are visible via dashboard
- TUI updates without manual refresh

## Design Document
See `docs/SMART_PROFILE_MANAGEMENT.md` for detailed design rationale, architecture decisions, and risk analysis.

## Sub-Epics
1. **Profile Health Foundation** - Core infrastructure
2. **Proactive Token Refresh** - Automatic token maintenance
3. **Usage Analytics** - Activity tracking and dashboard
4. **Runtime Enhancements** - Hot reload, signals
5. **Project Context** - Directory-profile associations

## Technical Approach
- SQLite for persistent storage (usage.db)
- JSON for lightweight metadata (health.json)
- Provider-specific OAuth handlers
- fsnotify for file watching","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:15:04.594984626-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:41:13.771976385-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:41:13.771976385-05:00","close_reason":"Planning complete: Design doc created at docs/SMART_PROFILE_MANAGEMENT.md. Implementation continues via sub-epics (caam-x0g, caam-e36, etc.)"}
-{"id":"caam-3b3","title":"Uninstall command","description":"Provide a clean way to remove caam and restore original state.

## Command
```
caam uninstall [flags]

Flags:
  --restore       Restore _original profiles before removing (default: true)
  --keep-vault    Keep vault directory (default: false)
  --keep-config   Keep config files (default: false)
  --force         Skip confirmation prompt
  --dry-run       Show what would be done without doing it
```

## Default Behavior (Interactive)
```
$ caam uninstall

caam uninstall will:
  ✓ Restore original auth for: claude, codex (gemini has no _original)
  ✓ Remove vault directory (~/.local/share/caam/vault/)
    - 3 claude profiles
    - 2 codex profiles  
  ✓ Remove config (~/.config/caam/)
  ✓ Remove activity database (~/.local/share/caam/caam.db)

Proceed? [y/N] y

Restoring claude auth from _original_claude... done
Restoring codex auth from _original_codex... done
Removing vault... done
Removing config... done
Removing database... done

caam has been uninstalled. Your original auth state has been restored.
```

## Edge Cases
- No _original exists for a tool → skip restore for that tool, warn user
- Vault doesn't exist → skip vault removal
- Restore fails → abort uninstall, report error

## Implementation
1. Create cmd/caam/cmd/uninstall.go
2. Add restore logic (iterate _original_* profiles)
3. Add cleanup logic (remove directories)
4. Add dry-run support
5. Add confirmation prompt

## Files
- cmd/caam/cmd/uninstall.go: New command
- cmd/caam/cmd/uninstall_test.go: Tests","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:45:28.458367802-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T21:45:28.458367802-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-3b3","depends_on_id":"caam-5fk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:45:39.688319849-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":44,"issue_id":"caam-3b3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Revised description - clarify operation order and fix profile naming:

## Purpose
Single command to completely reverse all caam changes and restore original state.
Critical for user trust: "I can always go back to how things were."

## Operation Order (CRITICAL)
The uninstall MUST happen in this exact order:

1. **RESTORE** original auth files from `_original` backup
2. **THEN** remove caam data (vault, config, database)

Why this order matters:
- If we delete first, we lose the backup needed to restore
- User would be left with whatever random profile was last active
- This violates the entire safety purpose

## Profile Naming Fix
Reference `_original` not `_original_claude` (see caam-5fk for rationale).

## Implementation

```bash
caam uninstall [--keep-backups] [--dry-run]
```

### Steps:
1. For each provider with an `_original` backup:
   a. Restore `_original` auth files to their canonical locations
   b. Verify restoration succeeded
2. If `--keep-backups` NOT set:
   a. Remove ~/.caam/vault/
   b. Remove ~/.caam/config/
   c. Remove ~/.caam/caam.db
3. Print summary of what was restored/removed

### Flags:
- `--keep-backups`: Restore originals but keep vault (user may want profiles later)
- `--dry-run`: Show what would happen without doing it
- `--force`: Skip confirmation prompt

## Files
- cmd/caam/cmd/uninstall.go: Main uninstall command
- internal/vault/restore.go: Restoration logic (separate from backup)

## Success Criteria
- Restores ALL original auth states before any deletion
- Single command, no manual steps required
- Dry-run shows exactly what will happen
- Works even if some providers have no _original (skips them with warning)
","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:06:35Z"}]}

 {"id":"caam-3ef","title":"Health status calculation logic","description":"# Health Status Calculation

## Purpose
Calculate profile health status (🟢🟡🔴) based on token expiry, errors, and penalties.

## Background (from codex-pool)
```go
score = headroom * planBonus * creditBonus - penalty
```

For caam v1, we simplify to status tiers with an internal numeric score.

## Technical Requirements

### Status Enum
```go
type HealthStatus int

const (
    StatusUnknown  HealthStatus = iota // ⚪ Cannot determine
    StatusHealthy                      // 🟢 Good to use
    StatusWarning                      // 🟡 Expiring soon or errors
    StatusCritical                     // 🔴 Expired or many errors
)

func (s HealthStatus) Icon() string
func (s HealthStatus) String() string
```

### Scoring Algorithm
```go
func CalculateHealth(h *ProfileHealth) (HealthStatus, float64) {
    score := 0.0
    now := time.Now()
    
    // Factor 1: Token expiry (primary)
    if h.TokenExpiresAt.IsZero() {
        // Unknown expiry - neutral
    } else if h.TokenExpiresAt.Before(now) {
        score -= 1.0  // Expired
    } else {
        ttl := h.TokenExpiresAt.Sub(now)
        switch {
        case ttl > time.Hour:
            score += 1.0
        case ttl > 15*time.Minute:
            score += 0.5
        default:
            // < 15 min: no bonus
        }
    }
    
    // Factor 2: Recent errors
    switch {
    case h.ErrorCount1h == 0:
        score += 0.3
    case h.ErrorCount1h <= 2:
        // Neutral
    default:
        score -= 0.5
    }
    
    // Factor 3: Plan type bonus
    switch h.PlanType {
    case "enterprise":
        score += 0.3
    case "pro", "team":
        score += 0.2
    }
    
    // Factor 4: Penalty (from errors, with decay)
    score -= h.Penalty
    
    // Convert to status
    status := StatusHealthy
    if score < 0 {
        status = StatusCritical
    } else if score < 0.5 {
        status = StatusWarning
    }
    
    return status, score
}
```

### Thresholds (Configurable)
```go
type HealthConfig struct {
    TokenExpiryWarningMinutes int     // Default: 60
    TokenExpiryCriticalMinutes int    // Default: 15
    ErrorCountWarning         int     // Default: 2
    ErrorCountCritical        int     // Default: 5
}
```

## User-Facing Display
```
🟢 Healthy   - Good to use
🟡 Warning   - Token expiring soon or recent errors
🔴 Critical  - Token expired or many errors
⚪ Unknown   - Cannot determine status
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HealthStatus enum with Icon() and String()
- [ ] CalculateHealth function with documented algorithm
- [ ] Configurable thresholds via HealthConfig
- [ ] Unit tests covering all status transitions
- [ ] Edge cases: zero time, negative penalty, nil health

## Files to Create/Modify
- `internal/health/status.go` (new)
- `internal/health/status_test.go` (new)

## Dependencies
- Health metadata storage (caam-xxx)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:17:57.789256368-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:14:39.883586788-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:14:39.883586788-05:00","close_reason":"Implementation complete in status.go - HealthStatus enum, CalculateHealth function, HealthConfig, and tests all passing. Committed as part of 2793c4c.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-3ef","depends_on_id":"caam-6gj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:27:22.207059052-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-3ef","depends_on_id":"caam-thb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:27:27.310848158-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-3ef","depends_on_id":"caam-0gb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:31:54.24697844-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-3nx","title":"EPIC: Data Safety & Recovery","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:42:40.84068493-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T21:42:40.84068493-05:00","comments":[{"id":36,"issue_id":"caam-3nx","author":"ubuntu","text":"Purpose: Ensure users can never lose their original authentication state and can fully reverse any changes made by caam. This is critical for user trust and adoption.

Background: Currently, caam has a dangerous gap: if a user runs caam activate foo without first backing up their current auth, the original files are overwritten and permanently lost. This violates the principle of least surprise and could cause real harm.

Scope: (1) Auto-backup of original state on first use, (2) Protected profile naming conventions, (3) Smart auto-backup before any switch, (4) Uninstall command.

Success Criteria: User can NEVER lose original auth through normal usage; Clear distinction between user/system profiles; Single command to reverse all changes; Zero additional friction.
","created_at":"2025-12-18T02:43:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-3w5","title":"Config schema: safety section","description":"Add safety configuration options to SPMConfig:

```yaml
safety:
  auto_backup_original: true  # Auto-backup on first activate
  auto_backup_before_switch: smart  # always|smart|never
  max_auto_backups: 5  # Rotate old _backup_* profiles
  protected_profile_prefix: '_'  # Profiles starting with this are protected
```

Files to modify:
- internal/config/spm_config.go: Add SafetyConfig struct
- internal/config/spm_config_test.go: Test defaults and validation

This is foundational - other safety features depend on these config options.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:43:31.755611107-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T21:43:31.755611107-05:00","comments":[{"id":39,"issue_id":"caam-3w5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Revised description - simplified config schema:

```yaml
safety:
  auto_backup_before_switch: smart  # "always" | "smart" | "never"
  max_auto_backups: 5               # Keep last N auto-backups, rotate older
```

## Changes from Original Design

REMOVED - `auto_backup_original`: This is now ALWAYS enabled, not configurable.
Rationale: Making it optional defeats the entire safety purpose. The one-time backup
of original state has zero downside and prevents catastrophic data loss.

REMOVED - `protected_profile_prefix`: Now hardcoded as `_`.
Rationale: Making this configurable adds complexity with no benefit. The `_` prefix
is a standard convention (like hidden files) and changing it could cause confusion.

## What Remains
- `auto_backup_before_switch`: Controls smart backup on every switch (optional)
- `max_auto_backups`: Limits accumulation of timestamped backups

## Files
- internal/config/spm_config.go: Add SafetyConfig struct (simplified)
- internal/config/spm_config_test.go: Test defaults and validation
","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:03:39Z"}]}

 {"id":"caam-ai6","title":"Research Gemini CLI headless authentication options","description":"# Research Gemini CLI Headless Auth

## Research Findings (Completed)

### Available Methods

#### 1. Device Code Flow (Recent Update - August 2025)
Gemini CLI now provides an auth URL to copy/paste, returning an activation code. This is similar to device code flow:
- CLI shows auth URL
- User opens URL on any device
- User gets code to enter back in CLI
- Works in headless environments!

**Status**: This appears to be a recent improvement. Need to verify current behavior.

#### 2. GEMINI_API_KEY Environment Variable
```bash
export GEMINI_API_KEY="your-api-key"
gemini "your prompt"
```
Direct API key authentication for Gemini API.

#### 3. GOOGLE_API_KEY Environment Variable
```bash
export GOOGLE_API_KEY="your-google-api-key"
gemini "your prompt"
```
Google Cloud API key authentication.

#### 4. Application Default Credentials (ADC)
```bash
export GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS="/path/to/service-account.json"
export GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT="your-project"
export GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION="us-central1"
gemini "your prompt"
```
For service account authentication - best for automation and CI/CD.

#### 5. BROWSER Workaround (Legacy)
Set BROWSER to a script that captures the auth URL:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "Auth URL: \$1" >> /tmp/gemini-auth-url.txt
```
Then manually complete auth on another device.

### Auth Files Location

~/.gemini/
├── google_accounts.json  # Active account email
├── oauth_creds.json      # OAuth credentials  
├── settings.json         # Auth type selection
├── installation_id       # Installation identifier
└── state.json            # Session state

### Implementation Path for caam

1. **Verify Device Code Flow**: Test if current gemini CLI supports URL-based auth
2. **AuthModeDeviceCode**: If supported, add to Gemini provider
3. **AuthModeAPIKey**: Support GEMINI_API_KEY and GOOGLE_API_KEY
4. **AuthModeServiceAccount**: Support ADC for automation

### Files to Modify

- internal/provider/gemini/gemini.go (if exists)
- Add multiple auth mode support

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Verify current gemini CLI device code behavior
- [ ] Implement API key mode with GEMINI_API_KEY
- [ ] Document service account setup for automation
- [ ] If device code works, implement caam integration

### References

- https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/issues/1696
- https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/discussions/2811
- https://google-gemini.github.io/gemini-cli/docs/get-started/authentication.html
- https://medium.com/@fourdollars/conquering-google-login-for-gemini-cli-on-headless-servers-3e9d2649790f","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:37:48.105233312-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:21.505993439-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:21.505993439-05:00","close_reason":"Research complete. Gemini has API key options (GEMINI_API_KEY) and recent device-code-like flow, but most reliable approach is vault transfer. Service accounts recommended for automation."}

-{"id":"caam-bgz","title":"Config schema: stealth section","description":"Add stealth configuration options to SPMConfig.

```yaml
stealth:
  enabled: false  # Master switch - all stealth features disabled by default
  
  delay:
    min_seconds: 30     # Minimum delay before switch completes
    max_seconds: 120    # Maximum delay (random between min-max)
    jitter_seconds: 30  # Additional random 0-N seconds added to delay
  
  cooldown:
    enabled: true       # Track and enforce cooldowns after limit hits
    codex_hours: 4      # Hours before codex account can be reused after limit
    claude_hours: 6     # Hours for claude
    gemini_hours: 4     # Hours for gemini
    default_hours: 4    # Fallback for unknown providers
  
  rotation:
    enabled: true       # Enable smart profile rotation
    prefer_healthy: true      # Weight toward profiles with fresh tokens
    avoid_recent_hours: 2     # Penalize profiles used in last N hours
    randomness: 0.2           # Random factor (0-1) in rotation scoring
```

## Validation Rules
- delay.min_seconds <= delay.max_seconds
- cooldown.*_hours >= 0
- rotation.randomness between 0 and 1

## Files
- internal/config/spm_config.go: Add StealthConfig struct with nested structs
- internal/config/spm_config_test.go: Test defaults and validation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:46:32.668918003-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T21:46:32.668918003-05:00","comments":[{"id":40,"issue_id":"caam-bgz","author":"ubuntu","text":"Revised description - fix confusing enabled flag structure:

```yaml
stealth:
  switch_delay:
    enabled: false          # Master switch for this feature
    min_seconds: 5
    max_seconds: 30
    show_countdown: true

  cooldown:
    enabled: false          # Master switch for this feature
    default_minutes: 60
    track_limit_hits: true

  rotation:
    enabled: false          # Master switch for this feature
    algorithm: "smart"      # "smart" | "round_robin" | "random"
```

## Changes from Original Design

REMOVED - Top-level `enabled: false` that conflicted with per-feature `enabled` flags.

Rationale: The original design had:
```yaml
stealth:
  enabled: false  # <-- This was confusing
  switch_delay:
    enabled: false  # <-- Redundant when parent is disabled
```

Users would be confused: "Do I need to set both? What if parent is true but child is false?"

NEW DESIGN: Each feature has its OWN `enabled` flag. No parent switch.
- Want just delays? Set `switch_delay.enabled: true`
- Want just cooldowns? Set `cooldown.enabled: true`
- Want everything? Enable each one individually

This is clearer, more explicit, and avoids the confusing interaction between parent/child enabled flags.

## Files
- internal/config/spm_config.go: Add StealthConfig struct (per-feature enabled)
- internal/config/spm_config_test.go: Test defaults and validation
","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:06:10Z"}]}

 {"id":"caam-by4","title":"Terminal UI (TUI) with Bubble Tea","description":"## Overview
A visual terminal interface for managing caam profiles. While the CLI is fully functional, a TUI provides:
- At-a-glance view of all profiles across providers
- Visual feedback on status (logged in, locked, active)
- Discoverable interface for new users
- Running session management

## Design (from original spec)
Layout:
- Left panel: Provider list (Codex / Claude / Gemini)
- Center panel: Profiles table (Name, Auth mode, Logged-in?, Last used, Notes)
- Right panel: Action pane + detail view for selected profile

Keybindings:
- ↑/↓: Select profile
- Enter: Run default command (launch CLI)
- l: Login/refresh auth
- o: Open account page in browser
- e: Edit profile (notes, browser config)
- d: Delete profile (with confirmation)
- /: Search/filter profiles
- Tab: Switch between providers
- q: Quit

## Implementation
Use Bubble Tea (bubbletea) with:
- bubbles for standard components (list, table, textinput)
- lipgloss for styling

## User Value
- Visual overview prevents mistakes (wrong account)
- Running sessions visible prevents data corruption
- Lower learning curve than CLI-only

## Consideration
TUI should be the default when running 'caam' with no args, but all functionality must remain available via CLI for scripting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:51:16.807075968-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:46:22.843793337-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:46:22.843793337-05:00","close_reason":"All TUI components implemented: scaffolding (caam-qpx), provider panel (caam-ta7), profiles table (caam-nmm), detail panel (caam-f9h), keyboard navigation (caam-j14). TUI is fully functional with three-panel layout and all keybindings wired up."}
 {"id":"caam-cfb","title":"Usage analytics CLI command","description":"## Purpose
Add `caam usage` command to display profile usage statistics from the command line.

## Background
Users should be able to see their usage patterns to understand which profiles
they use most, identify underutilized accounts, and optimize their workflow.

## Command Structure
```bash
# Summary view (default)
caam usage

# Detailed view for specific profile
caam usage --profile claude/work --detailed

# Time range filtering
caam usage --days 30
caam usage --since 2025-12-01

# Output formats
caam usage --json
caam usage --csv
```

## Example Output
```
$ caam usage
Profile Usage (last 7 days)
───────────────────────────────────────
claude/work         42 sessions   18.5 hrs
claude/personal     12 sessions    4.2 hrs
codex/main          28 sessions   12.1 hrs
gemini/default       3 sessions    0.5 hrs

$ caam usage --profile claude/work --detailed
Session History for claude/work
───────────────────────────────────────
2025-12-17 09:15  2.3 hrs  (active)
2025-12-16 14:20  1.8 hrs
2025-12-16 09:00  3.1 hrs
...

Total: 42 sessions, 18.5 hours over 7 days
Average session: 26 minutes
Most active day: Monday (12 sessions)
```

## Technical Implementation
```go
// cmd/caam/usage.go
var usageCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "usage",
    Short: "Display profile usage statistics",
    RunE:  runUsage,
}

func init() {
    usageCmd.Flags().StringP("profile", "p", "", "Profile to show (provider/name)")
    usageCmd.Flags().Bool("detailed", false, "Show detailed session history")
    usageCmd.Flags().Int("days", 7, "Number of days to include")
    usageCmd.Flags().String("since", "", "Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)")
    usageCmd.Flags().String("format", "table", "Output format: table, json, csv")
}

func runUsage(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    db, err := db.Open()
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    defer db.Close()
    
    // Query and format based on flags
}
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- cmd/caam/usage.go
- cmd/caam/usage_test.go
- Modify cmd/caam/root.go to add subcommand

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Summary shows all profiles with session count and duration
- [ ] Detailed view shows individual sessions for a profile
- [ ] Time range filtering works correctly
- [ ] JSON and CSV export work
- [ ] Handles empty database gracefully
- [ ] Performance acceptable for large activity logs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:22:53.646488393-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:11:12.338404628-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:11:12.338404628-05:00","close_reason":"Added caam usage command with summary/detailed views and json/csv output.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-cfb","depends_on_id":"caam-r36","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:28:57.057526979-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":21,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting caam usage command: implement cmd/caam/cmd/usage.go with summary + detailed profile view, since/days filtering, and table/json/csv output using internal/db.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:06:53Z"},{"id":22,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented  command with summary + per-profile detailed session history, days/since filtering, and table/json/csv output backed by SQLite activity_log. Added tests for empty DB, summary json, and detailed json.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:10:45Z"},{"id":23,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"(Follow-up) Implemented the caam usage command with summary + per-profile detailed session history, days/since filtering, and table/json/csv output backed by SQLite activity_log. Added tests for empty DB, summary json, and detailed json.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:11:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-ckk","title":"E2E: CLI command workflow tests","description":"End-to-end integration tests for CLI command workflows:

## Test Scenarios
1. `caam list` - List all profiles across providers
2. `caam backup <provider> <email>` - Backup current auth state
3. `caam restore <provider> <email>` - Restore saved profile
4. `caam delete <provider> <email>` - Delete a saved profile
5. `caam status` - Show current auth status for all providers

## Requirements
- Use t.TempDir() for isolated file system tests
- Real file operations (no mocks)
- Test actual cobra command execution
- Detailed logging at each step
- Test error cases (missing files, invalid profiles)

## Logging Format
Each test should log:
- [START] Test name and description
- [SETUP] Temp directory and fixture creation
- [EXEC] Command being executed
- [VERIFY] Expected vs actual results
- [CLEANUP] Resources cleaned up
- [PASS/FAIL] Final result with duration","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:52:40.357727069-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T02:15:21.938011493-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T02:15:21.938011493-05:00","close_reason":"Completed 9 E2E tests for CLI command workflows: list, backup, restore, delete, status, clear, profile workflow, invalid tool error handling, multi-provider isolation.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ckk","depends_on_id":"caam-7a2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:54:53.822477208-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-ckk","depends_on_id":"caam-aek","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:55:26.082995053-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
